mechanism_narrative: Declining unemployment rate (UNRATE) signals strengthening labor market conditions and rising consumer confidence, which drives increased discretionary spending and corporate earnings growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 4.9% in July 2016 reflects tightening labor market
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive 20-day forward returns occur in fewer than 55% of instances when UNRATE declines month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate remains near zero (FEDFUNDS < 0.5%), signaling accommodative monetary policy that sustains low discount rates for equity valuations and encourages equity allocation over bonds, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.39% in July 2016 reflects post-crisis accommodation
regime_hint: zero lower bound monetary policy regime
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day forward returns in > 45% of periods when FEDFUNDS < 0.5%, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month decline in retail sales (RSAFS) signals weakening consumer demand and reduced near-term corporate revenue expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days as equity prices adjust downward.
failure_condition: Negative 20-day forward returns occur in fewer than 50% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.02 when RSAFS declines exceed 1.5% month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) growth accelerates above its 6-month moving average, signaling strengthening manufacturing capacity utilization and supply-side expansion that supports corporate profit margins and equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 5-day forward returns occur in fewer than 52% of instances when INDPRO growth exceeds 0.3% month-over-month, or Rank IC < 0.01 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) and retail sales (RSAFS) both accelerate simultaneously, signaling broad-based demand strength across residential and consumer sectors that drives equity valuations higher through earnings growth expectations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST and RSAFS both show month-over-month growth > 1%
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1189K units in June 2016, RSAFS at 457.7B in July 2016
failure_condition: Positive 20-day forward returns occur in fewer than 58% of instances when both HOUST and RSAFS grow > 1% month-over-month, or the interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.05) in rolling regressions.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment (PAYEMS) growth decelerates below 100K new jobs per month while unemployment rate (UNRATE) remains stable, signaling labor market slack and reduced wage pressure that allows central banks to maintain accommodative policy, supporting equity valuations through lower discount rates.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS month-over-month growth < 100K and UNRATE change < 0.2pp
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 144.45M in July 2016, UNRATE at 4.9%
regime_hint: late-cycle labor market with policy accommodation
failure_condition: Positive 60-day forward returns occur in fewer than 55% of triggered instances, or the joint effect of PAYEMS deceleration and UNRATE stability does not produce significantly higher returns than either signal alone (interaction p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Inflation measured by CPI (CPIAUCSL) remains below 2% year-over-year while federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) is near zero, signaling that monetary policy is accommodative relative to inflation expectations, allowing equity risk premiums to compress and forward returns to be positive over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 239.93 in June 2016, FEDFUNDS at 0.39% in July 2016
regime_hint: low inflation with accommodative monetary policy
failure_condition: Positive 20-day forward returns occur in fewer than 60% of triggered instances, or the real interest rate signal (FEDFUNDS minus inflation) does not explain incremental variance beyond either component alone (R-squared improvement < 0.01).

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) decelerates below 2% annualized while housing starts (HOUST) remain elevated, signaling demand weakness in the broader economy but resilience in residential construction, creating a divergence that triggers equity repricing downward as investors recognize cyclical slowdown risks over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 annualized growth < 2% and HOUST > 1100K units
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 16575.1B in Q1 2016, HOUST at 1189K in June 2016
failure_condition: Negative 60-day forward returns occur in fewer than 50% of triggered instances, or the divergence signal (low GDP growth paired with high housing starts) does not produce significantly lower returns than GDP weakness alone (t-test p > 0.05).
